title: MD
biography: Dennis Slamon is an American oncologist who discovered the role of the HER2 gene in aggressive breast cancer and developed trastuzumab (Herceptin), a targeted therapy that has saved hundreds of thousands of lives. He is chief of hematology-oncology at UCLA.
aiSummary: Dennis Slamon is an oncology specialist with an H-index of 130 at UCLA. Discovered HER2 in breast cancer and developed Herceptin (trastuzumab). Based in Los Angeles, United States.

affiliations.role: Director of Clinical and Translational Research

timeline.title: Published HER2 discovery
timeline.description: Published landmark paper linking HER2 gene amplification to aggressive breast cancer

timeline.title: FDA approved trastuzumab (Herceptin)
timeline.description: Herceptin became the first HER2-targeted therapy approved for breast cancer

timeline.title: Lasker Award
timeline.description: Received the Lasker-DeBakey Clinical Medical Research Award for HER2 work
